> **Version:** 1.0
> **Status:** Living document — updated when ADR conventions change
> **Related:** [Issue template](issue-template.md) | [Documentation registry](../registry.md)

# ADR template

> This template defines the required structure for all Architecture Decision Records in this repository.
> Copy this file to `docs/adrs/ADR-NNN-short-title.md` before editing.
> Remove all HTML comments and guidance text before committing.

---

<!-- ================================================================
FILENAME GUIDANCE
- Format: ADR-NNN-short-title.md
- NNN: zero-padded sequential number (001, 002, ...)
- short-title: lowercase, hyphen-separated, 3–6 words
- Example: ADR-002-cloudflare-cdn-over-cloud-cdn.md
================================================================ -->

<!-- ================================================================
STATUS GUIDANCE
Accepted        — decision is in effect
Superseded      — replaced by a later ADR; add "Superseded by ADR-NNN" to the status line
Rejected        — considered and not adopted; document why for future reference
================================================================ -->

> **Status:** <!-- Accepted | Superseded by ADR-NNN | Rejected -->
> **Date:** <!-- YYYY-MM-DD -->
> **Related:** <!-- Links to architecture.md, affected docs, or superseded ADR -->

# ADR-NNN: [Decision title]

## Context

<!-- What is the situation that led to this decision?
     What constraints, requirements, or observations made a decision necessary?
     State facts only — no opinions or recommendations here.
     2–5 sentences or a short list. -->

## Options considered

<!-- Table of options evaluated. Include the option chosen.
     Keep descriptions short — one sentence per option. -->

| Option | Notes |
|---|---|
| Option A | |
| Option B | |
| Option C (chosen) | |

## Decision

<!-- One sentence stating what was decided.
     Bold the key technology or approach chosen. -->

## Rationale

<!-- Why was this option chosen over the alternatives?
     Address the specific concerns that make this decision non-obvious.
     This section earns its length — write as much as the decision requires, no more. -->

## Consequences

<!-- Bullet list of outcomes: what becomes easier, harder, or different as a result.
     Include both positive and negative consequences.
     Be concrete — avoid vague statements like "more complex". -->
